vBRIEFInfo.author: agent:oz
vBRIEFInfo.description: Tkinter GUI — JSON persistence for designs + recent-files menu

plan.title: Saved designs — JSON persistence + recent-files menu

narratives.Overview: The qr23mf Tkinter GUI (src/qr23mf/gui.py) currently loses every setting the user enters as soon as the window closes — payload, EC level, plate dimensions, QR placement, module style, finish, output format, and every text label have to be re-entered on next launch. This brief adds a lightweight JSON save/load flow (File menu + keyboard shortcuts) plus a persistent recent-files list, so a frequent user can name and round-trip their designs and the GUI remembers the most recent 8 across sessions.
narratives.Acceptance: 1) A new **File** menu is added to the root Tk window with entries: **New** (resets all fields to module defaults), **Open…** (⌘O / Ctrl+O; asks for a .json path and loads every field), **Open Recent ▶** (submenu of up to 8 most-recently-used paths; selecting one loads it; each entry shows the basename tooltipped with the absolute path; a trailing **Clear Recent** entry empties the list), **Save** (⌘S / Ctrl+S; writes the current design to the current file path or falls back to Save As if none), **Save As…** (asks for a .json path and writes). 2) A new module `src/qr23mf/design_io.py` exposes `Design` (dataclass mirroring `_gather_design`'s return fields plus output format), `design_to_dict(d) -> dict[str, object]`, `design_from_dict(d) -> Design`, `save_design(design, path)` and `load_design(path)`. The JSON schema is `{"version": 1, "payload": str, "ec": 'L'|'M'|'Q'|'H', "plate": {...}, "qr": {...}, "module_style": 'square'|'dot', "finish": 'extruded'|'flush'|'sunken', "output": 'threemf'|'svg', "text_labels": [{...}, ...]}` — unknown top-level keys are tolerated with a `warnings.warn` so future additive changes don't break older versions; missing keys fall back to module defaults. 3) A `Recents` helper (inside `design_io.py` or a sibling module) persists the recent list at `~/.config/qr23mf/recents.json` on POSIX and `%APPDATA%\qr23mf\recents.json` on Windows, MRU-ordered, capped at 8, deduplicated on `Path.resolve()`, missing paths pruned on load. 4) Error paths: invalid JSON, wrong types, missing required top-level keys, and unreadable file all surface via `messagebox.showerror` with a clear message and leave the current in-GUI state untouched. 5) The window title reflects the current file: `qr23mf — <basename>` when a path is loaded, `qr23mf — Untitled*` when there are unsaved changes against no file (or a modified file), trailing `*` indicating dirty state. 6) Tests under `tests/test_design_io.py` cover: JSON round-trip for an every-field-populated design, unknown-key tolerance, missing-key defaults, invalid JSON rejection, recents trimming to 8, recents path-normalisation (relative vs absolute collapse), and MRU ordering after repeated adds. 7) The gate stays green under `task check`; coverage on `design_io.py` ≥95%; `gui.py` stays excluded from coverage but the new File-menu wiring is smoke-tested headlessly (same pattern used by the auto-update terminal tests).
narratives.Scope: New files: `src/qr23mf/design_io.py` (Design dataclass + JSON codec + Recents helper), `tests/test_design_io.py`. Edits: `src/qr23mf/gui.py` — build and attach a Tk menubar with **File** menu, wire actions to `design_io.save_design` / `load_design`, implement dirty-state tracking via `tk.StringVar` traces on every form variable, drive window title from (path, dirty) tuple. Non-goals: cloud sync, design templates / presets (tracked separately), auto-save, macOS-native Cocoa menu integration beyond the default Tk wrapping, undo/redo stack. These would each be follow-up briefs.
narratives.Traces: FR-4 (sensible defaults — new designs start from module defaults), FR-5 (UX improvement to the GUI surface).

references.description: Existing Tkinter GUI this brief extends
Source of truth for Design field defaults (GeometryParams, QrPlacement, TextLabel)
